prompt: [Module 1]  A radiologic technologist is reviewing a new digital radiography system's documentation. The system uses the IEC 62494-1 standard for Exposure Index. According to this standard, if the detector receives an air kerma of 2.0 μGy, what Exposure Index value would the system display?
[Module 1]  What does Exposure Index (EI) primarily reflect in digital radiography?

choices.A: Patient absorbed dose
choices.B: Scatter radiation within the field
choices.C: Detector exposure level
choices.D: Tube current setting (mA)

prompt: [Module 1]  In digital radiography, why does visual image brightness alone not confirm that the patient received an appropriate radiation dose?

choices.A: The display monitor is always miscalibrated
choices.B: Brightness is normalized during post-processing
choices.C: Radiologists manually adjust brightness after review
choices.D: Images are always underexposed in digital systems

prompt: [Module 1]  Which statement is TRUE regarding the relationship between Exposure Index (EI) and patient absorbed dose?

choices.A: EI is a direct measurement of patient absorbed dose
choices.B: EI increases only when patient absorbed dose decreases
choices.C: EI reflects detector exposure and does not directly represent patient absorbed dose
choices.D: EI is unaffected by technique factors such as kVp or mAs

prompt: [Module 1]  Two patients undergo the same abdomen projection using a digital system with a target EI (EI_T) of 400. Both images record an EI of 400. One patient is significantly larger. Which statement BEST describes the dose implication?

choices.A: The larger patient received a lower absorbed dose because EI was the same
choices.B: The larger patient received the same absorbed dose because EI was the same
choices.C: The larger patient likely received a higher absorbed dose because more exposure was required to achieve EI_T
choices.D: Absorbed dose cannot vary when EI values match

prompt: [Module 1]  A technologist notices that EI values across multiple exams are consistently above the target EI_T. What is the MOST likely risk associated with this trend?

choices.A: Increased image noise due to underexposure
choices.B: More repeat images due to motion blur
choices.C: Exposure creep leading to unnecessary patient dose
choices.D: Loss of spatial resolution due to high kVp

prompt: [Module 2]  In the IEC 62494-1 standard, the Deviation Index (DI) is designed to represent:

choices.A: The patient’s entrance skin exposure relative to baseline
choices.B: How far the actual detector exposure deviates from the target exposure
choices.C: The percentage of image noise present in a study
choices.D: A fixed correction factor applied during post-processing

prompt: [Module 2]  A DI value of +2 indicates that detector exposure is approximately how far ABOVE the target?

choices.A: About 25% above target
choices.B: About 50% above target
choices.C: About 60% above target
choices.D: About 100% above target

prompt: [Module 2]  A DI value of -1 indicates the detector exposure is closest to:

choices.A: Approximately 20% above target
choices.B: Approximately 10% above target
choices.C: Approximately 20% below target
choices.D: Approximately 50% below target

prompt: [Module 2]  Which of the following DI ranges is MOST appropriate for routine diagnostic imaging in a well-controlled department (assuming proper positioning and collimation)?
[Module 2]  A technologist performs a portable chest exam with poor collimation and includes a large area of unattenuated air outside the patient. How can this MOST affect EI/DI accuracy?

choices.A: EI/DI will always decrease because scatter increases
choices.B: EI/DI will always increase because the detector receives more exposure
choices.C: EI/DI can be artificially skewed because field recognition/histogram analysis is impacted
choices.D: EI/DI cannot be affected by collimation

prompt: [Module 2]  Which statement BEST describes the difference between EI and DI?

choices.A: EI and DI are identical values reported in different units
choices.B: EI reports detector exposure, while DI reports the deviation from a target exposure
choices.C: EI reports patient dose, while DI reports detector dose
choices.D: DI is only used in computed radiography (CR), not digital radiography (DR)

prompt: [Module 3]  In digital radiography, which action MOST directly supports ALARA while maintaining image quality?

choices.A: Increasing mAs until noise is eliminated regardless of EI/DI
choices.B: Using a fixed technique for all patients to simplify workflow
choices.C: Using technique charts and monitoring EI/DI to stay near target exposures
choices.D: Avoiding collimation to ensure anatomy is not cut off

prompt: [Module 3]  Which statement is TRUE regarding kVp and patient dose in digital radiography when technique is adjusted appropriately?

choices.A: Increasing kVp always increases patient dose
choices.B: Decreasing kVp always decreases patient dose
choices.C: Increasing kVp can reduce dose if mAs is reduced while maintaining receptor exposure
choices.D: kVp has no effect on patient dose

prompt: [Module 3]  A technologist increases SID for a projection but does not adjust technique factors. What is the MOST likely EI/DI outcome?

choices.A: EI/DI increases because the detector receives more exposure
choices.B: EI/DI decreases because the detector receives less exposure
choices.C: EI/DI remains unchanged because SID does not affect exposure
choices.D: EI/DI becomes invalid due to post-processing normalization

prompt: [Module 3]  Which situation is MOST likely to cause exposure creep in a digital radiography department?

choices.A: Technologists rely on EI/DI trends and correct technique consistently
choices.B: Radiologists request lower dose images and accept increased noise
choices.C: Technologists increase technique to reduce noise because post-processing masks overexposure
choices.D: Departments strictly enforce target EI values with QA review

prompt: [Module 4]  A department wants to reduce unnecessary patient dose and standardize exposures. What is the BEST first step in implementing an EI/DI QA program?

choices.A: Eliminate technique charts and let technologists adjust freely
choices.B: Track only repeat rates and ignore EI/DI values
choices.C: Establish documented target EI (EI_T) values and acceptable DI ranges by exam type
choices.D: Only evaluate image brightness as the primary metric

prompt: [Module 4]  After identifying that EI/DI values are trending high for portable chests, what is the BEST corrective action approach?

choices.A: Ignore trends if image quality appears acceptable
choices.B: Reduce all portable chest techniques by 50% immediately without review
choices.C: Review technique selection, positioning/collimation practices, and implement targeted retraining with follow-up monitoring
choices.D: Increase technique to reduce noise complaints

prompt: [Module 4]  Which metric provides the MOST direct early warning sign of exposure creep?

choices.A: Repeat rate only
choices.B: Radiologist satisfaction score
choices.C: EI/DI trend analysis over time
choices.D: Patient wait time

prompt: [Module 4]  A department’s EI/DI review shows frequent outliers for lateral lumbar spine exams. Which factor MOST commonly contributes to inconsistent EI/DI in this projection?

choices.A: Use of grids always lowers EI
choices.B: Patient thickness variation and improper AEC chamber selection
choices.C: Digital post-processing eliminates EI variation
choices.D: SID is the only contributing factor

prompt: [Module 4]  A quality improvement team is using EI/DI data to document compliance with ALARA. Which practice BEST supports defensible documentation?

choices.A: Storing only the final images without exposure metadata
choices.B: Recording EI/DI values occasionally when there is a complaint
choices.C: Maintaining routine EI/DI trend reports and corrective action logs
choices.D: Deleting outlier exams so averages look better

prompt: [Modules 1-4]  Which statement BEST summarizes why EI/DI monitoring is essential in digital radiography?

choices.A: EI/DI guarantees patient dose is always low
choices.B: EI/DI replaces the need for technique charts
choices.C: EI/DI helps detect overexposure masked by post-processing and supports consistent ALARA practice
choices.D: EI/DI determines image contrast and spatial resolution directly

prompt: [Modules 1-4]  A technologist wants to reduce patient dose on a routine projection while maintaining acceptable image quality. Which approach BEST aligns with ALARA?

choices.A: Increase mAs until no noise is visible
choices.B: Use technique charts and adjust factors while monitoring EI/DI to stay near targets
choices.C: Ignore EI/DI and judge only by image brightness
choices.D: Collimate widely to avoid clipping anatomy

prompt: [Modules 1-4]  A department has properly implemented an EI/DI QA program, including documented EI_T values, monthly trend review, and corrective action records. Which statement BEST describes why visual image quality assessment alone is insufficient for this program to be effective?

choices.A: PACS systems do not store EI/DI values in the DICOM header, so there is no way to retrieve exposure data programmatically for trend analysis
choices.B: Visual assessment confirms patient dose accurately when performed by an experienced technologist who has been trained in image quality standards
choices.C: Post-processing adjusts display appearance independently of detector exposure level, so visual review cannot detect overexposure
choices.D: Radiologists are not trained to evaluate image quality from a dose perspective and cannot reliably distinguish overexposed from correctly exposed images
